mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5%, the Fed is in an accommodative stance relative to price pressures. This regime produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as investors price in sustained liquidity and real asset appreciation.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-09-15, FEDFUNDS=0.38% and CPIAUCSL inflation elevated
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when trigger condition is met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum. This sustained job creation supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,560 thousand as of August 2021, reflecting post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of monthly instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,100 thousand units annualized indicate robust residential construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence. This construction activity multiplies through the economy via employment and materials demand, supporting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,099 thousand as of July 2021, near threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) when HOUST > 1,100, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.3% signal supply-side weakness or demand destruction. This contraction in manufacturing output predicts negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 (i.e., signal direction reverses).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, signaling weakening consumer demand and labor market deterioration. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447,308 million and UNRATE at 4.9% as of August 2021
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.0% annualized, indicating a tight labor market within sustained expansion. This regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as wage growth and profit margins expand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% and GDPC1 at 16,455.1 billion as of Q2 2021
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 2.0%) when both conditions hold, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.5% in a single month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 4.5% year-over-year, the Fed is tightening into inflation. This policy shift produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as discount rates rise and real returns compress.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS monthly increase > 0.5pp AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.38% and CPIAUCSL at 238.1 as of September 2021
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 (signal direction reverses).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 250,000 persons monthly, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion. This dual acceleration produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as production capacity and earnings growth accelerate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.4% AND PAYEMS monthly growth > 250,000
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.85 and PAYEMS at 143,560 thousand as of July-August 2021
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
